mnemonics: For-Loops Iterate Over Items, While-Loops Wait for Condition
keyPoints: For loops are used to iterate over sequences (like lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets, or strings).
While loops repeat a block of code as long as a specified condition is True.
The 'range()' function is commonly used with for loops to generate a sequence of numbers.
commonMistakes: Creating an infinite loop by forgetting to update the variable in the while loop condition.
Using the wrong loop type (for vs. while) for the task, making code less readable or efficient.
Off-by-one errors when using range(), forgetting it starts at 0 and stops before the end number.
